summary: REQ-33 is partially addressed by introducing a denylist and a feature-filtering helper in SubscriptionPanel, but the diff excerpt does not show that the filter is actually applied to the rendered tier features list. The changes also appear to introduce unrelated behavior changes (notably a simplified admin check) and modify non-requested project metadata/summary files.

missing_requirements.requirement: Remove the support-related phrases "Community support", "Priority Support", and "Dedicated Support" so they no longer appear anywhere in the Subscription tab UI (including the tier features list rendering).
missing_requirements.reason: Although a denylist and filter function are added, the provided diff snippet is truncated before any evidence that the Subscription UI actually uses `filterSupportFeatures(...)` when rendering tier feature lists. Without evidence of integration, it's not confirmed that the strings no longer appear in the UI.
evidence: frontend/src/components/settings/SubscriptionPanel.tsx (truncated) — defines SUPPORT_FEATURES_DENYLIST and filterSupportFeatures but no shown usage in rendering

hallucinated_features.description: Changed/introduced admin-gating logic in the Subscription panel via a simplified `isAdmin = identity !== null` check (with comment indicating it is simplified).
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest explicitly lists as a non-goal: "Do not modify routing, admin gating, or other Settings tabs beyond the Subscription UI text removal." The diff shows a potentially behavior-changing admin determination in SubscriptionPanel.
evidence: frontend/src/components/settings/SubscriptionPanel.tsx (truncated) — `const isAdmin = identity !== null; // Simplified; in production, check actual admin role`

hallucinated_features.description: Updated project metadata/summary files unrelated to removing support-related phrases from the Subscription tab UI.
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest only asks to remove specific phrases from Subscription tab UI. Modifying high-level state/summary artifacts is not requested and may indicate scope creep.
